rationale: The citing paper uses Manchanda et al. (2006) in more than a passing list citation. It is invoked in the core literature positioning as prior work on online advertising effectiveness, and the authors define their contribution as adding the offline-regulation context to that stream. The paper also explicitly references Manchanda et al.'s website purchase outcome approach when motivating why this study relies on purchase-intent survey measures in alcohol settings.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Manchanda et al. (2006) as an empirical benchmark in the online display advertising effectiveness literature. It builds from that baseline by examining how offline advertising bans moderate online ad effectiveness and by adapting the outcome measure to a context where direct online purchase data are less available.
